hypothesis: Incorporating explicit metal coordination environment descriptors—derived from local geometry fingerprints of metal nodes—as auxiliary training targets during uMLIP fine-tuning on MOF-relevant data will improve prediction accuracy for flexible MOFs undergoing large-amplitude breathing transitions more than simply increasing the quantity of non-equilibrium training structures, because the current performance bottleneck for such materials lies in misrepresentation of coordination bond directionality rather than insufficient potential energy surface coverage.
